scSidekick — Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps

07_spatial.Rmd

Overview

scSidekick’s three spatial plotting functions mirror their scRNA-seq counterparts but render expression and metadata directly on the tissue image:

  • PlotSpatialDimPlots() — color spots by a metadata variable (e.g. cluster, condition, spatial domain). Long-form alias: GenerateSpatialDimMaps().
  • PlotSpatialFeaturePlots() — color spots by gene expression. Long-form alias: GenerateSpatialFeatureMaps().
  • PlotMasterMaps() — combined figure: tissue image + gene expression panels + cluster-colored UMAP side by side. Long-form alias: GenerateMasterGeneMaps().

All three functions accept group.by, split.by, layout_method, and output_dir with the same conventions as the scRNA-seq equivalents.

Demo dataset — mouse brain (Visium) 

library(scSidekick)
library(Seurat)
library(SeuratData)

InstallData("stxBrain")
brain <- LoadData("stxBrain", type = "anterior1")

# Standard processing
brain <- NormalizeData(brain)
brain <- FindVariableFeatures(brain)
brain <- ScaleData(brain)
brain <- RunPCA(brain)
brain <- RunUMAP(brain, dims = 1:30)
brain <- FindNeighbors(brain, dims = 1:30)
brain <- FindClusters(brain, resolution = 0.5)

# Fake condition column for split demos
set.seed(42)
brain$Condition <- sample(c("WT","KO"), ncol(brain), replace = TRUE)

# Register colors
brain <- PrepObject(brain,
  variables   = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"),
  group.by    = "seurat_clusters",
  output_dir  = tempdir(),
  object_name = "MouseBrain")

After PrepObject(), the cluster palette is stored and will appear consistently across all spatial figures.

PlotSpatialDimPlots

1. Basic spatial cluster map 

PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by = "seurat_clusters")

Each spot on the tissue section is colored by its cluster assignment. The tissue H&E image is shown as a grey-scale background. A legend maps colors to cluster numbers. The palette comes from PrepObject.

2. Multiple variables — iterate

Passing a vector to group.by produces one spatial map per variable.

PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"))

Two figures are generated: one showing spatial cluster distribution, one showing which spots are annotated WT vs KO.

3. Uniform spot size

By default Visium spot sizes are scaled by local spot density. Set uniform_size = TRUE to use a fixed, equal size for all spots — useful for comparing two sections where spot density differs.

PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by     = "seurat_clusters",
  uniform_size = TRUE)

4. Metadata-driven layout with layout_method

layout_method = "metadata" uses row_var to stack multiple spatial maps vertically, one row per level of row_var. Useful when you have multiple tissue sections or ROIs stored in the same object.

PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by      = "seurat_clusters",
  layout_method = "metadata",
  row_var       = "Condition")

Each row of the output corresponds to one condition level. Columns would correspond to levels of split.by if provided.

5. Colors from PrepObject vs custom 

# Colors from PrepObject (default)
PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by = "seurat_clusters")

# Override with a custom palette
my_cols <- GetColors(brain, "seurat_clusters")
my_cols["0"] <- "#FF0000"  # re-color cluster 0

PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  colors   = my_cols)

6. Saving to disk 

PlotSpatialDimPlots(brain,
  group.by    = "seurat_clusters",
  output_dir  = tempdir(),
  object_name = "MouseBrain")

PlotSpatialFeaturePlots

1. Basic spatial gene expression 

PlotSpatialFeaturePlots(brain,
  features = c("Hpca", "Ttr"))

Each gene generates one spatial map. Spot color encodes normalized expression from grey (zero) to the high-expression color (default: dark red). Hpca marks hippocampal neurons; Ttr marks the choroid plexus — both should appear in their expected anatomical locations.

2. Metadata-driven layout 

PlotSpatialFeaturePlots(brain,
  features      = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),
  layout_method = "metadata",
  row_var       = "Condition")

Rows correspond to the levels of Condition. Each gene’s spatial pattern is shown once per condition. This layout is most useful for comparing expression between biological groups when each group occupies a distinct tissue section in the same object.

3. Saving to disk 

PlotSpatialFeaturePlots(brain,
  features    = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),
  output_dir  = tempdir(),
  object_name = "MouseBrain",
  subset_name = "Anterior1")

Files are named by gene: e.g. MouseBrain_Anterior1_spatial_Hpca.pdf.

PlotMasterMaps

PlotMasterMaps is a composite figure function: for each gene it assembles a multi-panel page containing the tissue spatial map, a UMAP colored by the specified group.by, and a UMAP colored by gene expression — all on one page, ready for a supplementary figure or lab presentation.

1. Basic master map 

PlotMasterMaps(brain,
  features   = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),
  group.by   = "seurat_clusters",
  output_dir = tempdir())

Each gene produces a PDF page with three panels arranged horizontally: 1. Spatial expression map (spot colors = expression level) 2. UMAP colored by group.by (cluster) for orientation 3. UMAP colored by gene expression

This lets you simultaneously see where in the tissue the gene is expressed and which clusters drive that expression.

2. Multiple group.by 

PlotMasterMaps(brain,
  features   = c("Hpca", "Ttr"),
  group.by   = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"),
  output_dir = tempdir())

Each combination of gene × group.by variable produces its own panel page. With 2 genes and 2 group.by variables, you get 4 pages.

3. Custom gene expression color scale 

PlotMasterMaps(brain,
  features    = "Hpca",
  group.by    = "seurat_clusters",
  gene_colors = c("lightgrey", "#006837"),
  output_dir  = tempdir(),
  object_name = "MouseBrain")

gene_colors is a two-element vector: [1] is the zero-expression color, [2] is the high-expression color. The default is grey → dark red.

Parameter reference — PlotSpatialDimPlots

Parameter Type Default Description
group.by character (vector) from PrepObject Variable(s) to color spots by
layout_method character "auto" "auto" or "metadata"
row_var character NULL Row-grouping variable (metadata layout)
colors named character NULL Override palette
uniform_size logical FALSE Use equal spot size
output_dir character NULL Directory for saving
object_name character "" Label used in filenames

Parameter reference — PlotSpatialFeaturePlots

Parameter Type Default Description
features character vector required Genes to plot
layout_method character "auto" "auto" or "metadata"
row_var character NULL Row-grouping variable
output_dir character NULL Directory for saving
object_name character "" Label used in filenames
subset_name character "" Sub-label used in filenames

Parameter reference — PlotMasterMaps

Parameter Type Default Description
features character vector required Genes to visualize
group.by character (vector) "seurat_clusters" Variable(s) for the UMAP orientation panel
colors named character NULL Override UMAP cluster colors
gene_colors character (length 2) c("grey","darkred") Zero and max expression colors
output_dir character required Directory for saving
object_name character "" Label used in filenames

See also

  • Vignette 01: PrepObject — register spatial cluster colors
  • Vignette 03: PlotDimPlots — UMAP-only equivalent for scRNA-seq
  • Vignette 04: PlotFeaturePlots — scRNA-seq gene expression on UMAP

sessionInfo()
